From English (EN) To Russian (RU)
> Я случайно наткнулся на Морименса в Каппе и удивился, что не знал о таком прекрасном проекте. Одного взгляда на Долл мне хватило, чтобы попробовать. Я знал, что ни в одной другой игре не будет таких потрясающих персонажей—Я очень ценю этот художественный стиль. То

From English (EN) To Russian (RU)
> Способности персонажей блестяще проработаны—объединять и изучать их было невероятно увлекательно, и даже сейчас, спустя 600+ часов, я понимаю, что еще так многому предстоит научиться. Почти каждый персонаж здесь может сыграть свою роль и понять содержание; даже ст

From English (EN) To Russian (RU)
> Возьмем, к примеру, Наутилу: в ее сообщениях ее проблемы с памятью разыгрываются ради комедии, например: «Ха-ха, она забыла, кому пишет, забыла, чего хочет, и прочая глупость» В игре, посвященной темам памяти, вы ожидаете более глубокого обращения с персонажем с т

content:
Thank you—for meeting us at such a special moment.

Reading about your struggle through Chapter 6 really moved me. To be honest, some of us on the team felt the same during the hardest moments—we wanted to give up too. But in the end, we didn’t—did we? Just like you.

And if we want to tell more of G Tulu’s story, we first need to make sure we can properly develop and release his full content.

Year 3 is our goal.  <:realm_aequor:1437283645051375777> 
See you then.

Thank you—and welcome, new Keeper!

As the original designer of our core gameplay, I’m genuinely happy to hear that you enjoy the mechanics and the strategic side of Morimens.

To be candid, because we lacked experience with this genre early on (and had to move very fast), there are still some regrets in our balance and design philosophy. Going forward, we hope to gradually fix and improve those areas—ideally without simply “nerfing” things—so the game becomes even more fun to play.

We’ll keep working hard. Thank you for being here!

Thank you for loving our story, atmosphere, and character designs!

We’re also very excited to explore more possibilities of cosmic horror in future updates. We hope the upcoming stories and events—especially those focusing on side characters—will continue to surprise you.

Thank you for staying with us and supporting the narrative. More to come!
谢谢你的反馈！https://discord.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1471099912392282396 我们应该已经修复了这个问题。很抱歉影响了你的体验！
Btw, I forgot to say this in my initial reply. To everyone at biav studio, thank you for not giving up. <:Ramona_love:1459077541829546007>
貓還在龜速回，他真的，我哭死

We’re truly honored that Morimens helped you fall in love with this genre—our combat designers will be so happy to hear that!

And regarding future behind-the-scenes stories: +1 vote for Uvhash noted. I’ve written it down!<:uvhash_love:1468257700986228758>
大概就是睡醒起来发现还没回完(？)

We’ll keep cooking—absolutely!<:Karen_Happy_Birthday:1459076984251486413> 

But our team is also starting to take some time off over the next few days. Chinese New Year is a very important traditional holiday here: many of us work in big cities far from our hometowns, and this is often the one time of year we can truly go home—just like in the latest story.

After burning ourselves out all year, we want everyone to get some real rest during this break. Then we’ll come back and continue cooking！

Thank you so much for your recognition and support.

Looking back, Chapter 9 really was a miracle in many ways. Our resources were very limited at the time, but we still wanted to capture a long, difficult battle—one filled with memories and weight.<:gamekey:1448943277117145140> 

So I told the team: let’s build the boss around our past themes and create different “versions” of it. It worked out wonderfully, and it allowed us to deliver the experience we wanted within our constraints.

I also hoped those battles could give more characters a chance to appear—even some who didn’t get a dedicated chapter of their own. Bringing them back on stage like that felt really special.

And Frank’s rearrangements of the music are absolutely not to be missed.

We’ll keep moving forward with the same passion. Thank you for being here with us.

Thank you for the encouragement!

A lot of people have shared the same idea, and we’re personally very interested in making an official artbook too. It’s a huge project though, and we’d want to do it properly—something worthy of Morimens—so we’ll need enough manpower and the right schedule.

If an artbook becomes feasible in the future, we’ll be sure to let everyone know.

Thank you so much for replying!! I'm very happy to know you would always do your utmost best to offer us the best experience possible.

Regardless of outcome, I want to thank again the entire dev team for bringing morimens to life! I will keep cheering you on, hopefully for many years to come!

Thank you so much for loving our work!

<:Hameln_Discontent:1468258981444063303> +1 vote for Hamelyn noted. We’ve always wanted our characters to feel deep and multifaceted—both in the story and in how they play—and we’ll keep working hard to deliver.

oh why, thank _you_ Light.  It's honestly great to see the head of the studio behind the game interact with the community so casually and actively. ~~Although you didn't promise more Faint content so obviously this game is rotten to the core~~ <:fail:1356083552923422770> (joking <:Ramona_love:1459077541829546007>)

<:Tawil_loneliness:1460158738110349372>  Tawil, <:bow:1356053037159944473> Thais, and <:wish:1356084702468899117> Lily’s stories were all written by our lead writer, Jiyue! What a lovely coincidence—I’ll be sure to pass your words along to her.

We’re also really glad you’ve been enjoying the HSR mode. We’ll keep experimenting and try to bring more varied game modes in the future.

Clementine +1 noted! She’s one of those characters we had lore for early on but couldn’t release at the time—she even showed up back in A1C1—so we’re very happy we finally got to explore her story further in A2C2.

And lastly, thank you for the warm encouragement. We’ll keep moving forward, and we’re grateful to have you with us.
